data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) × child B5 (f12d: Cut the claim/close gates over to the workflow path, signing intact)]

Walked all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B6 7e48, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumptions + scope overlap, B5↔0408). The other six modes read benign — footnoted.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDING 1 — Conflicting assumptions + scope overlap: B5 (this child) is CLOSED + SIGNED asserting `gate_engine` defaults to "workflow" with a live-correct plan-review cutover, but PR #42 reverted that default to "bespoke" because B5's own cutover left the workflow plan-review verify/coach steps without `{{plan}}` on the LIVE path (→ INDETERMINATE). Sibling 0408 (open) silently re-owns the same default=workflow end-state that B5 attests as DONE.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- criteria:
  • parent AC4 ("the bespoke orchestrator path is cut over to the workflow path … DEFAULTING to the workflow path; the close/claim gates keep working")
  • parent Success Criterion ("plan-review … run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate")
  • B5 (this child) AC1 ("…dispatch through the engine workflow path behind a config flag, DEFAULTING to the workflow path")
  • 0408 AC2/AC4 (live review-plan returns a real PASS/BLOCK; re-flip default to workflow)

- location: `verify.gate_engine` default (src/rebar/config.py:276–283); the workflow plan-review verify/coach prompt steps (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py RunnerAgentStep; gates/plan-review.yaml); bespoke listing builders in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (pass2_verify/pass4_coach).

- evidence:
  • B5's signed completion-verification PASS comment asserts: "src/rebar/config.py:276: `gate_engine: str = \"workflow\"` is the default" and "AC1 … defaulting to workflow … MET."
  • The LIVE tree CONTRADICTS that signed claim: config.py:283 reads `gate_engine: str = "bespoke"`, and the docstring (config.py:276–282) states the workflow plan-review verify "degrades to INDETERMINATE live — a B2 offline blind spot the B5 cutover surfaced," and that flipping the default to "workflow" is "gated on the plan-review plumbing fix (follow-up)."
  • RunnerAgentStep (runs.py:131–137) supplies ONLY {ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path} into resolve_prompt — confirming the plan-review verifier/coach prompts' required `{{plan}}` (and findings/surviving listing) are NOT provided on the live path.
  • 0408 Why + B-RETIRE comment (1782505678304851001): "PR #42 had to flip the DEFAULT gate_engine back to bespoke because the workflow PLAN-REVIEW verify/coach steps don't supply {{plan}}/findings on the live path … bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate."
  • B5's own verifier hedged: "No full end-to-end live signing test over the workflow path exists" — the offline/structural verification that signed B5 could not see this live blind spot.

- scenarios:
  1. A reviewer reconciling the roster sees B5 SIGNED-complete for "default = workflow / live gate cut over," yet 0408 (open) says that exact deliverable is broken and must be re-done — two siblings own the same config-default end-state with contradictory completion status.
  2. If B5's signature is trusted and 0408 is deprioritized/dropped, the epic ships with `gate_engine=bespoke` permanently — plan-review NEVER actually runs on the workflow substrate live, silently defeating the parent's central Success Criterion despite a green/signed sibling.
  3. B-RETIRE (961b) removes the bespoke run_review path + the B5 fallback flag. B5's signed evidence would suggest the workflow path is already the proven sole-capable gate; a reader trusting B5's signature (rather than 0408's completion) could let B-RETIRE proceed, deleting the only WORKING (bespoke) plan-review gate. (B-RETIRE's own comment already corrects this by re-gating on 0408 — but only because the inconsistency was caught manually, not because B5's face reflects the reversion.)

- impact: The sibling set is internally inconsistent on a load-bearing claim. B5's signed AC1/end-state ("workflow is the working default") is not true in the tree; B5 over-scoped its signed verdict (it set default=workflow on an unsound live path, then PR #42 reverted it), and 0408 silently re-owns the corrective without B5 being annotated/reopened. The epic's primary Success Criterion (plan-review running live on the one workflow substrate) hinges entirely on 0408 landing, yet a signed sibling already attests it done. This is a real ordering/overlap artifact, not duplication of independent work.

- checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes … conflicting assumptions … scope overlap … each detected mode is a finding."

- suggested_fix: Make the inconsistency explicit and acknowledged on the plan: (a) annotate B5 (f12d) that its AC1 "defaulting to workflow" held only structurally/offline and was reverted by PR #42 pending 0408 — its signed default=workflow claim no longer reflects the tree; (b) surface the 0408→f12d `discovered_from` link as a forward pointer on B5's face so no reader treats B5's signature as proof the workflow gate is the working default; state in 0408 that it COMPLETES B5's AC1/AC2 on the live path (verify/coach `{{plan}}`+findings plumbing) rather than as an independent task; (c) ensure B-RETIRE (961b) keys its bespoke-removal precondition off 0408's completion, not B5's signed status, before deleting the bespoke path (B-RETIRE already added this — keep it).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state — B5 mutates surfaces also touched by B2 (gates/plan-review.yaml), B3 (gates/completion-verification.yaml + the executor terminal-output fix B3 flagged for B5), and 0408 (RunnerAgentStep, gate_engine default, passes.py builders). But the sharing is sequenced (B5 after B4) and the B3→B5 hand-offs (terminal-arm verdict surfacing; INDETERMINATE-on-outage) are acknowledged on B5's face. The contradictory VALUE of the gate_engine default is captured in Finding 1; the remaining co-edits are ordered, not hidden concurrent mutation.

(2) Conflicting assumptions (other) — B5's completion-verification cutover is live-correct (B3's completion-verifier prompt needs only {{ticket_id}}+{{ticket_context}}, both supplied by runs.py; B5's signed completion-verifier PASS of produce_completion_verdict + close fail-closed semantics hold). Only the plan-review half is contradicted — captured in Finding 1. No separate completion-side conflict.

(3) Dependency gap — B5 correctly depends on B4 parity ("parity must hold first") and consumes B2/B1 (ProductionBatchRunner) + B3 (completion-verification.yaml). B5 blocks B-RETIRE (961b) + B6 (7e48) (deps present: f12d blocks 7e48 and 961b). No missing dependency.

(5) Ordering violation — B5 is correctly after B4 (parity) and before B-RETIRE (fallback/bespoke removal). The only sequencing wrinkle (B5 prematurely defaulted to workflow before the live plumbing existed) is the substance of Finding 1, not a separate ordering finding.

(6) Consumer impact — flipping the live claim gate to workflow changes gate behaviour, but interface tests pin REBAR_VERIFY_GATE_ENGINE=bespoke to keep validating the fallback, and signing wrappers are unchanged (byte-compatible attestations). The live INDETERMINATE regression IS a consumer impact, but it manifested as the gate_engine reversion already captured in Finding 1 — not reported twice.

(7) Residual references — B5 preserves the bespoke path behind the flag (no removal); B-RETIRE keeps the shared passes/sizing/attest machinery the workflow still uses. No dangling reference to a removed symbol.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because the LIVE tree (config.py:283 = "bespoke"; runs.py supplies only 3 vars, no {{plan}}) OBJECTIVELY contradicts B5's signed assertion (config.py = "workflow", AC1 MET), and B5 + 0408 own the same default=workflow end-state with opposite completion status. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule.
